name: Floem
description: SolidJS-inspired signals, powers the Lapce editor

maintenance-status.comment: Last push 2026-04-20; regular commit activity in the last 6 months

maturity.comment: Pre-1.0 (0.2.0); README explicitly states 'still maturing' with occasional breaking changes expected before v1.0. Used in production by Lapce and Lapdev.

corporate-backing.comment: Developed under the Lapce GitHub organization (lap.dev); community-driven rather than funded by a traditional corporation.

implementation.comment: Pure Rust with wgpu/Vello/Skia/tiny-skia rendering backends; no C/C++ toolkit dependency.

rendering-mode.comment: README: 'The view tree is constructed only once, safeguarding you from accidentally creating a bottleneck.' Retained view tree updated via fine-grained signals.

reactivity-model.comment: Fine-grained reactivity inspired by leptos_reactive (signal primitives).

thread-model.comment: UI must run on the main thread (winit-based).

renderer.comment: Default feature uses Vger (wgpu); optional backends include Vello (wgpu) and Skia; tiny-skia serves as a CPU/software fallback.

gpu-acceleration.comment: GPU rendering via wgpu is the default; tiny-skia CPU fallback kicks in only when no GPU is available.

hidpi-support.comment: Winit + wgpu pipeline handles HiDPI; window-scale example demonstrates scale-factor handling.

vector-rendering.comment: Svg widget plus Vello/Skia vector renderers.

custom-shaders.comment: Repo contains a 'webgpu' example which embeds custom wgpu rendering, but injecting custom shaders into the UI renderer itself is not documented as a supported API.

embedded-custom-rendering.comment: Official 'webgpu' example demonstrates embedding custom wgpu content inside the UI.

linux-x11.comment: Linux supported via winit, which includes the X11 backend.

linux-wayland.comment: Linux supported via winit, which includes a native Wayland backend.

ios.comment: iOS listed as 'planned support' on the project homepage; not officially supported today.

android.comment: Android listed as 'planned support' on the project homepage. A 'navigation-stack-mobile' example exists but mobile is not an officially supported target.

web-wasm.comment: Web support via WebGPU is described as experimental on lap.dev/floem, not production-ready.

embedded-no-std.comment: No no_std or microcontroller support documented.

authoring-style.comment: Views composed via Rust functions and tuples with a builder-style .style() closure; no DSL or macro markup language.

hot-reload.comment: No hot reload documented; changes require recompile.

layout-system.comment: Flexbox and Grid provided via the Taffy crate.

styling-approach.comment: .style(|s| ...) closures for inline styling; global themes and classes with light/dark presets via a built-in design system.

theme-switching.comment: README advertises 'Global themes' with light/dark mode support; 'themes' example demonstrates runtime switching.

accessibility.comment: 2025 survey tested Floem with Windows Narrator and reported 'the complete lack of accessibility or IME support is the real issue.' No AccessKit integration is documented as of 2026-04-24.

internationalization.comment: Built-in localization via the Fluent crate with runtime language switching; 'localization' example in the repo.

widget-breadth.comment: Common widgets well covered (button, label, text input, checkbox, radio, slider, toggle, dropdown, tabs, scroll, virtual-list, rich-text, text-editor, SVG, image). Lacks first-party table/tree/chart widgets.

data-grid-table.comment: No dedicated table widget; virtual_list/virtual_stack handle virtualization but are not grid/table abstractions.

rich-text-markdown.comment: RichText view + text_editor module provide rich text rendering; no dedicated Markdown widget, but the rich-text primitive covers styled runs.

native-menus.comment: Uses the muda crate (Windows/macOS) for native menu bars and context menus; widget gallery includes a Context Menu demo.

file-dialogs.comment: Uses the rfd crate for native file dialogs; 'files' example demonstrates usage.

custom-widgets.comment: Custom views implemented via the View trait; documentation exists but requires understanding Floem's view/id internals. Functional composition is pleasant for composite widgets but the tuple-based API has a practical upper bound (noted in external surveys).

async-runtime-compatible.comment: Explicit tokio feature flag in Cargo.toml; official 'tokio-timer' and 'async-resource' examples. Non-tokio runtimes not specifically advertised.

system-tray.comment: No system tray support documented in floem or the muda dependency surface as exposed by Floem.

notifications.comment: No built-in OS notification API surfaced by Floem; users would integrate a separate crate.

clipboard.comment: Clipboard integration via platform crates (clipboard-win on Windows, objc bindings on macOS); widget gallery has a dedicated Clipboard demo.

drag-and-drop.comment: Widget gallery includes 'Draggable' and 'Dropped File' demos; drag-and-drop events exposed through the view API.

embedded-webview.comment: No embedded webview widget; Floem is a native UI stack only.

multi-window.comment: Multi-window supported via winit; window-icon/window-scale/window-size/handle_window_close examples demonstrate multiple top-level windows.

binary-size.comment: Not measured in this research pass; no published hello-world binary size figures.

startup-time.comment: Not measured in this research pass.

memory-footprint.comment: Not measured in this research pass.

idle-redraw.comment: Retained view tree with signal-driven invalidation; redraws only when tracked state changes.

dependency-count.comment: ~38 direct dependencies per lib.rs; transitive tree lands in the 50–200 crate 'moderate' bracket (not precisely counted via cargo tree in this pass).

documentation-quality.comment: docs.floem.dev overview site + docs.rs API reference + 31 in-repo examples. No comprehensive book/tutorial series; adequate but not exhaustive.

api-ergonomics.comment: Idiomatic Rust with signal primitives and closure-based .style() API. External review notes a practical limit of 16 sibling views in a container tuple; functional but with rough edges.

examples-count.comment: 31 example crates in the official repo (counted 2026-04-24).

interactive-playground.comment: No online playground or hosted demo gallery; widget-gallery must be run locally via cargo.

ide-tooling.comment: Pure-Rust source; relies on rust-analyzer. No framework-specific LSP, formatter, or inspector beyond the runtime element inspector (counted separately).

dev-tools-inspector.comment: Built-in element inspector for debugging layout ('inspired by browser dev tools').

last-release.comment: floem 0.2.0 published 2024-11-14; no newer crates.io release as of 2026-04-24 despite ongoing main-branch commits.

notable-production-users.comment: Floem was extracted from/drives the Lapce editor; lap.dev also lists Lapdev as a user.

community-size.comment: 201 forks, 20 subscribers; active Discord per README but contributor count is modest. Fits the small bracket rather than medium or solo.

ecosystem-crates.comment: Third-party floem-ui-kit exists; broader ecosystem of themes/widgets/integrations is sparse.
